calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Accepting Resignation Of Trustees And For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-01-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 156578 Estate of Martha Sutton Smith.

Appearances: Spencer Weisbroth for and with Felix Smith.

Mr. Weisbroth stated that the case settled, that the settlement agreement called for resignation of two of the three Trustees, that he filed a declaration yesterday setting forth all of the assets and asked that bonds be waived. The matters were taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition Seeking Approval Of Court-Supervised Settlement
Off Calendar May-19-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Seth Schwartz and Spencer Weisbroth for and with Felix Smith (Petitioner). Adrian Sawyer for and with Nathan Smith, for Nathan Smith, Jr. and for Jonathan Smith (Objectors). Betty Epstein for Lawrence Smith and Martha Downs (Co-Trustees).

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to valuation of the property, the issue of remainderment to the trust, which Parties attended/participated in the settlement conferences, all interests being protected and the terms of the deal/sale. The Court stated that the decision would be based on the merits of the matter. Ms. Epstein suggested that no decision be made regarding the appointment of the Successor Trustee. The Court took the matter under submission.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

Petition Seeking Approval Of Court-Supervised Settlement
Off Calendar May-05-2009 Continued To May-19-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Seth Schwartz and Spencer Weisbroth for and with Felix Smith (Petitioner). Betty Epstein for Lawrence Smith and Martha Downs (Co-Trustees). Adrian sawyer for Nathan Smith, Sr., Nathan Smith, Jr. and Jonathan Smith (Objectors).

The Parties argued as to whether the matter should go before Judge Wiss as she held the settlement conference or remain in the Probate department. Mr. Sawyer stated that he objected to the settlement. Mr. Schwartz stated that the objection had to do with the terms of the settlement and that the probate department was better prepared to deal with trust issues. The matter was continued to May 19, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Maria Lertora, CSR#11935. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Petition For Instructions Re Sale Or In The Alternative Motion For Stay
Off Calendar Sep-10-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stacie Nelson for Larry Smith and Martha Downs (Co-Trustees). Seth Schwartz for Felix Smith (Trustee/Beneficiary).

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions. After hearing argument, the Court stated that the Motion For Stay Of The Petition For Instructions was denied. The Court denied Ms. Nelson's request to give reason for the ruling. Mr. Schwartz will prepare and present the order to Ms. Nelson for approval as to form and submit it to the court.

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust And For Removal Of Trustees
Off Calendar Jul-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Seth Schwartz for Felix Smith. Ken Philpot for Larry Smith and for Martha Downs. Kyle Childress for Michael Smith and for Geoffrey Smith.

Mr. Schwartz stated that discovery had closed and asked the Court to transfer the matter to the civil department to have it set for trial. Mr. Philpot stated that he disagreed, that the matter was not ready to be set for trial, that the pending Motion for Summary Judgment would be dispositive of the case, that discovery was not complete and objected to the matter being referred to the civil department. Mr. Childress stated that it was time for the matter to move along. The Court signed the order referring the case to the department of the Presiding Judge.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Lee.

Petition To Convert Trust To Unitrust And For Instructions To Trustees For The Sale Of Frog Lake.
Off Calendar Jul-14-2008 Text Ruling

Ex Parte Application For Order Shortening Time
Off Calendar Jul-02-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stacey Nelson for Lawrence Smith and Martha Downs (Respoondents). Seth Schwartz for Felix Smith (Petitioner).

The parties presented oral argument to support there positions. The Petition for Order Shortening Time was denied.

Judge: Raymond Arata. Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto CSR#5229. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust And For Removal Of Trustees
Off Calendar May-19-2008 Continued To Jul-14-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Matter was heard with case #290596 (The Lawrence H. Smithe Trust).
Appearances: Set Schwartz for Felix Smith, Jr., Co-Trustee; Ken Philpot for Lawrence Smith and Martha Downs, Co-Trustees; Jean Johnson for Michael Smith and Geoffrey Smith.

Mr. Philpot requested 60 days to complete Discovery. Mr. Schwartz stated that there were no resolutions from the settlement discussion with Mr. Philpot and requested transferring the case to the Civil division for trial setting. Ms. Johnson stated that the request extending time is just a delay, parties were not close to settling and the matter was not moving forward. The Court continued the matter to July 14, 2008 for status.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy Guitron.

Petition To Convert Trust To Unitrust And For Instructions To Trustees For The Sale Of Frog Lake.
Off Calendar May-19-2008 Continued To Jul-14-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust And For Removal Of Trustees
Off Calendar Feb-04-2008 Continued To May-19-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case #290596 (The Lawrence H. Smith Trust).
Appearances: Jim Feutz for Craig Smith (Beneficiary). Ken Philpot for Larry Smith and Martha Downs (Tustees). Seth Schwartz and Spencer Weisbroth for Felix Smith. Kyle Childress for Michael Smith and Jeffrey Smith.

Mr. Feutz stated that he was being relieved as counsel, and that Craig Smith was his only client. Mr. Philpot pointed out that Frog Lake was held in three trusts, that only two were before the Court, and stated that a neutral independant trustee should be appointed. Mr. Schwartz stated that the depositions of Lawrence Smith and Martha Downs were not completed, and that discovery would be completed by the end of May. Mr. Childress expressed that he would like to see a decison and that he had no objection to Felix being the Trustee. The parties presented argument reagarding the sale of Frog Lake, the removal of the Trsutees, the conversion of the trust to a unitrust, and the posibility of a partition action. The Court informed all parties that because there was no agreement on all of the details, there was no way to move forward on the unitrust without evidence. The Court will not permit Felix to be the sole Trustee, and needs to know who will step in. The court urged all parties to work this out, to encourage all parties not present to work it out and suggested including someone who can value Frog Lake and help with the taxes. The matter was continued to May 19, 2008 for status. Mr. Schwartz will notice all parties in Pro Per and all parties being represented by counsel.
Judge Pro Tem: Sue Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR# 11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Hill.

Petition To Convert Trust To Unitrust And For Instructions To Trustees For The Sale Of Frog Lake.
Off Calendar Feb-04-2008 Continued To May-19-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Ex Prte Order Compelling Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust And For Removal Of Trustees
Off Calendar Nov-05-2007 Continued To Feb-04-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ken Philpot for Trustee. Kyle Childress for Michael Smith and Geoffrey Smith. Spencer Weisbroth and Seth Schwartz for Felix Smith. Jim Feutz for Martha Downs and Larry Smith.

Mr. Childress requested a 2 week continuance to file a joinder. He reported that he will join with Felix Smith, that his clients were for selling the property and that he had no opportunity to respond. Mr. Philpot stated that he just received notice Friday and requested guidance from the Court. He expressed there is no reason to litigate, that they have a buyer for Frog Lake, and that he is willing to step down when an institutional trustee is in place. Mr. Feutz concurs with Mr. Philpot. Mr. Weisbroth stated that Mr. Smith was entitled to have a full hearing on these issues, to take depositions , that the findings and tentative ruling would be unfair and requested the matter be set for further status. Mr. Philpot stated that he is willing to stipulate to not withdraw the listing agreement and that the depositions would be completed in January. The Court expressed that is was unclear as to the purpose of the depositions, that the first step would be to sell Frog Lake and suggested mediation. All parties agreed to continue the matter to February 04, 2008.

Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Felicia Smith

Petition To Convert Trust To Unitrust And For Instructions To Trustees For The Sale Of Frog Lake.
Off Calendar Nov-05-2007 Continued To Feb-04-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Relief From Breach Of Trust; For Conversion Of Trust To Unitrust; For Order Enjoining Trustee Etc.
Off Calendar Jul-09-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Ken Philpot appearing for trustees. Seth Schwartz appearing for Felix Smith. Kyle Childress appearing for two remainder beneficiaries. Jim Feutz appearing for five remainder beneficiaries. Mr. Feutz wanted to seek professional mediation. Mr. Schwartz wanted to have the property sold. Mr. Childress agreed with Mr. Schwartz that the property should be sold. Mr. Philpot stated his argument. Court is denying the petition without prejudice to any interested person to file a petition to instruct the trustees to sell the property. Mr. Schwartz can file a petition for Felix as a beneficiary to instruct the trustees to sell the property. He will need to file separate petitions for the other trusts in separate files. Issue on conversion to unitrust discussed. Court wanted parties to work on terms of the the unitrust that is not objected when property is sold. They are also to talk about mechanism for valuation of the property. Mr. Philpot will prepare the order and send it to other counsel for approval as to form.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: R. Drocco.
